summary: Decompiled and named most of the DevText subsystem in src/melee/un/un_2FC9, replacing many address-style un_80302xxx symbols with typed DevText APIs, adding the DevText struct and flags, and wiring draw/setup/print/list-management behavior through baselib CObj/GObj/particle helpers. The PR also renamed lb_8000C160 to memzero and propagated that clearer name across callers, renamed particle helpers to DrawRectangle and DrawASCII, and fixed a GObj callback prototype so an explicit cast in sislib was no longer needed. decomp.dev reported +1316 matched bytes and many DevText functions reaching 100%, while the author clarified that reported broken functions were only renames.
what_changed: Replaced address-based DevText-related symbols in symbols.txt with names such as DevText_StrLen, DevText_NumToStr, DevText_GetGObj, DevText_InitPool, DevText_Remove, DevText_SetupCObj, DevText_Draw, DevText_DrawAll, DevText_CreateCObj, DevText_Setup, DevText_AddToList, DevText_Show, DevText_Create, DevText_EraseFirstLine, DevText_SetCursorXY, DevText_SetCursorX, DevText_HideCursor, DevText_ShowBackground, DevText_HideBackground, DevText_ShowText, DevText_HideText, DevText_SetScale, DevText_SetXY, DevText_SetTextColor, DevText_SetBGColor, DevText_Erase, DevText_Print, DevText_PrintInt, and DevText_Printf.

Added C implementations for many previously stubbed DevText functions in un_2FC9.c, including string length, integer-to-string conversion, pool initialization, list insertion/removal, CObj setup, draw-all dispatch, text erase/scroll, cursor/scale/color setters, print, print-int, and printf support.

Introduced struct DevText in src/melee/un/types.h with position, size, cursor, scale, background color, four text colors, id, line width, flags, current color bitfield, buffer pointer, and prev/next links.
Defined DevText flag macros: DEVTEXT_FLAG_HIDETEXT, DEVTEXT_FLAG_HIDEBACKGROUND, DEVTEXT_FLAG_NOWRAP, and DEVTEXT_FLAG_SHOWCURSOR.
Replaced old forward declarations for un_80302AB0_t and un_80302B10_t with typedef struct DevText DevText.
Added DevText static globals in un_2FC9.static.h, including devtext_CObjDesc, devtext_pool[32], devtext_drawlist, devtext_gobj, devtext_cobj, setup parameter globals, and devtext_poolhead.
Renamed lb_8000C160 to memzero in symbols, lb_00B0.c, lb_00B0.h, and callers in gm, gr, mp, and un code.
Renamed baselib particle symbols hsd_80391580 and hsd_80391664 to DrawRectangle and DrawASCII and gave them typed prototypes using GXColor.
Changed HSD_GObj_803910D8 to take an int renderpass parameter in gobj.c/h, allowing sislib.c to call GObj_SetupGXLinkMax without a cast.
Left DevText_Create as an unresolved decomp marker in un_2FC9.c while declaring it in the public header, consistent with the PR title matching 'most' rather than all of DevText.
smart_moves: Recovered the central DevText struct before typing the API, which made the small flag/cursor/scale/color functions self-documenting and replaced two anonymous partial structs with one coherent type.
Used descriptive DevText_* names consistently across symbols.txt, headers, and implementations, making decomp.dev rename regressions easy to distinguish from actual match breakage.
Promoted the zero-fill helper to the semantic name memzero and propagated it across unrelated systems, reducing future confusion around lb_8000C160 call sites.
Typed low-level drawing helpers as DrawRectangle and DrawASCII, which clarified DevText_Draw without needing to fully decompile those particle functions in this PR.
Adjusted HSD_GObj_803910D8's callback signature rather than leaving call sites with casts, improving header truth and reducing future matching friction.
Preserved matching-sensitive compiler behavior with #pragma dont_inline around DevText_Setup and small inline helpers such as DevText_Clamp and DevText_AdvanceLine.
Kept the still-unmatched DevText_Create as a named placeholder instead of forcing an incomplete implementation, allowing surrounding functions and headers to progress safely.
decomp_lessons: When a cluster of tiny bit-manipulation functions all touch the same byte offset, recovering the owning struct and flag names can unlock many accurate names and prototypes at once.
A decomp.dev report that shows functions dropping from 100% to 0 after a PR may be harmless when the affected entries are symbol renames; document that explicitly in PR comments.
Global utility renames should be propagated through symbols.txt, source, and headers together to avoid stale declarations and false breakage reports.
For draw subsystems, naming external helpers with typed prototypes can make the caller match/read better even if the helpers themselves remain undecompiled.
Callback prototypes should match actual call signatures; removing casts can expose the true API and simplify later work.
It is acceptable to make partial progress on a subsystem if the remaining unmatched function is clearly named and left as a decomp marker.
naming_conventions: Subsystem functions use PascalCase-style prefix DevText_ followed by an action or noun phrase, e.g. DevText_SetupCObj, DevText_DrawAll, DevText_EraseFirstLine, DevText_PrintInt.
Recovered static globals use lowercase devtext_ prefixes, e.g. devtext_pool, devtext_drawlist, devtext_gobj, devtext_cobj, devtext_poolhead.
DevText flag macros use all-caps DEVTEXT_FLAG_* names with positive semantic labels like HIDETEXT, HIDEBACKGROUND, NOWRAP, and SHOWCURSOR.
A not-yet-understood DevText function retained address context as DevText_80302AC0 rather than inventing a behavior name.
The generic zeroing helper was renamed from lb_8000C160 to memzero.
Baselib drawing helpers were renamed from hsd_80391580/hsd_80391664 to DrawRectangle and DrawASCII.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around DevText_Setup to preserve desired code generation.
Used inline helpers DevText_Clamp and DevText_AdvanceLine to express repeated logic while staying matching-sensitive.
Included PAD_STACK(8) in DevText_DrawAll, indicating stack-shape preservation for matching.
Kept DevText_Create as /// #DevText_Create instead of adding a nonmatching approximation.
Introduced precise prototypes for DrawRectangle, DrawASCII, hsd_80391A04, and HSD_GObj_803910D8 so call sites no longer needed UNK_PARAMS or casts.
Updated symbols.txt in lockstep with code-level renames so decomp tooling recognized function identity despite renamed symbols.
review_feedback: decomp.dev reported matched code improved to 26.66% (+0.03%, +1316 bytes).

decomp.dev listed 100% matches for DevText_StrLen, DevText_SetupCObj, DevText_DrawAll, DevText_CreateCObj, DevText_AddToList, DevText_Show, DevText_EraseFirstLine, DevText_SetCursorXY, DevText_SetCursorX, DevText_SetXY, DevText_StoreColorIndex, DevText_SetTextColor, DevText_SetBGColor, DevText_Erase, and DevText_PrintInt.

decomp.dev also reported partial progress for DevText_NumToStr at 96.20%, DevText_InitPool at 27.85%, DevText_Remove at 57.69%, DevText_Draw at 7.58%, DevText_Setup at 76.32%, DevText_Print at 77.03%, and DevText_Printf at 92.68%.
The author responded to a broken-functions report by stating: 'These are all function renames.'
No line-level human review comments were present in the provided slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish or improve DevText_Create in src/melee/un/un_2FC9.c.
Search for DevText_80302AC0 to determine its real behavior and whether it should be renamed.
Review later changes to DevText_Draw, DevText_InitPool, DevText_Remove, DevText_Setup, DevText_Print, and DevText_Printf for improved match percentages.
Search for further typing or decompilation of DrawRectangle, DrawASCII, and hsd_80391A04 in sysdolphin/baselib/particle.
Check whether memzero remains the accepted project-wide name for lb_8000C160-like zero-fill behavior.
